prompt: Set up a Local RentOps Billing & Booking Platform that enables property managers to take offline bookings for rentable assets (e.g., storage units, small equipment, short-term spaces), automatically bill tenants, and keep finance records consistent from order to receipt. The system serves four roles: Administrator (configures the system and manages users), Property Manager (creates inventory, schedules, and resolves exceptions), Tenant (books, pays, and receives receipts), and Finance Clerk (runs reconciliation and exports). The React web interface provides a mobile-friendly login with username and password, a tenant portal for placing a booking, rescheduling, canceling, and viewing bills, and a manager console for availability calendars, no-show handling, and payment/refund actions. When a tenant places an order, the UI must show real-time availability feedback and a “hold timer” countdown; a hold lasts 10 minutes before releasing inventory if not confirmed. Cancellation rules are displayed before confirmation: free cancellation up to 24 hours before start time, otherwise a 20% fee, and a no-show triggers a 50% penalty plus the first day’s rent. Billing screens must support initial, recurring (monthly on the 1st at 9:00 AM local time), supplemental, and penalty bills, with receipts/invoices downloadable as PDF and clearly marked as “Paid,” “Partially Refunded,” or “Voided.” A notification and subscription center lets each user opt in/out per event, set Do Not Disturb (default 9:00 PM–8:00 AM), and view delivery status; all notifications are delivered in-app only to keep the project fully offline. If a display terminal is enabled, managers can register terminals, group them by location, and schedule playlist rotations with multilingual and accessibility-friendly layouts, including offline package transfers with pause/resume. The backend uses Symfony to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the React frontend, enforcing server-side validation, concurrency control, and financial correctness, with MySQL for durable storage of accounts, devices, bookings, inventory, bills, payments, refunds, notifications, terminal assets, and a fund ledger. Authentication is strictly local: username/password with bcrypt, JWT access tokens (15-minute expiry) plus refresh tokens (14-day expiry), device sessions capped at 5 active devices per account, and forced logout on password change; any “third-party/mobile/email login” concepts are explicitly disabled to preserve offline operation. Booking placement must be idempotent using a client-generated request key with a 24-hour dedupe window; overselling is prevented via inventory pre-deduction and row-level locking, and high-concurrency throttling is applied per inventory item (e.g., token bucket allowing 30 order attempts per minute per item). Payment flows are implemented as an offline “external processor simulator” module: signature verification uses local shared secrets, callbacks are idempotent, amounts and currency must exactly match the open bill total (allowing partial payments only when configured), and refunds support full or partial reversal with immutable audit trails. Reconciliation tools compare bill state, payment state, and ledger entries daily, flagging mismatches for Finance review and enabling CSV exports for accounting. System configuration includes dictionaries, feature toggles, template text for in-app notifications, API versioning, and environment profiles; operations support includes health checks, baseline metrics (latency, error rate, queue depth), local log-based error tracking, and backup/restore with encrypted database dumps (AES-256) plus masking of sensitive fields in UI and logs (e.g., only last 4 characters of IDs).
